title: OWASP Top 10 Flashcards
tools_required: Index cards, Anki, or text editor
when_to_use: Foundational knowledge for all web application security work

steps.title: Create Cards
steps.instruction: Write each OWASP Top 10 category on a card (or digital equivalent)
steps.tip: Use the OWASP 2021 version as the current standard

steps.title: Add Descriptions
steps.instruction: Add a one-sentence description of the vulnerability class
steps.tip: Keep it concise. If you can't explain it in one sentence, you don't understand it yet

steps.title: Add Examples
steps.instruction: Add one real-world example (e.g., SQLi: attacker extracts database through login form)
steps.tip: Real examples make abstract concepts concrete and memorable

steps.title: Add Risk Levels
steps.instruction: Add risk level: Critical, High, Medium
steps.tip: Risk depends on context. Injection is critical in most cases; cryptographic failures depend on the data

steps.title: Add Defences
steps.instruction: Add the primary defence (e.g., SQLi: parameterised queries)
steps.tip: Know the primary defence cold. This shows you understand not just the attack but the fix

steps.title: Daily Review
steps.instruction: Review daily until you can name and explain all 10 from memory
steps.tip: Use spaced repetition (Anki). You should be able to recite all 10 in under 2 minutes
